subject: Fix NPE in UpdateReq.reconfigure()
commit_message:
Fix NPE in UpdateReq.reconfigure()

While running CSIT to test heartbeat changes with multiple PAPs,
observed an NPE in UpdateReq.reconfigure().  Modified the code to
always use deployment lists instead of null lists.

Issue-ID: POLICY-3460
Change-Id: I884e091817e88309330139a01d060e286bd42008
Signed-off-by: Jim Hahn <jrh3@att.com>
(cherry picked from commit a87b9d9e191b4dee5d821db55d1ff4fd70b8d73a)

entries.message: Patch Set 1: Cherry Picked from branch master.

Patch Set 1:

This NPE was recently seen in Bell pre-production environment with Honolulu version of PF. Hence, doing a cherry pick to make the fix available in honolulu branch.

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/rtdv3-global-verify-master/10026/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/rtdv3-global-verify-master/10026

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-pap-honolulu-verify-java/36/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/policy-pap-honolulu-verify-java/36

Patch Set 1:

Are there any side effects that you can think of in single PAP mode?  
It seems to me that the change is trivial but would like to make sure.    
The deploy/undeploy lists are handled differently between jakarta and honolulu (honolulu carries duplicated lists for policies/deploy/undeploy).   
Can it present any issues?
Does it go to Istanbul as well?

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> Are there any side effects that you can think of in single PAP mode?  
> It seems to me that the change is trivial but would like to make sure.    
> The deploy/undeploy lists are handled differently between jakarta and honolulu (honolulu carries duplicated lists for policies/deploy/undeploy).   
> Can it present any issues?
> Does it go to Istanbul as well?

Good Question Jorge,
Looking at the change, it won't create any issues, and will make sure that the NPE is avoided. Basically it does this

private <T> List<T> alwaysList(List<T> list) {
        return (list != null ? list : Collections.emptyList());
    }

Also, this change seems to be compatible with both single & multi-cluster PAP.
It was already released as part of Istanbul. So, only back porting to honolulu is needed.

> Good Question Jorge,
> Looking at the change, it won't create any issues, and will make sure that the NPE is avoided. Basically it does this
> 
> private <T> List<T> alwaysList(List<T> list) {
>         return (list != null ? list : Collections.emptyList());
>     }
> 
> Also, this change seems to be compatible with both single & multi-cluster PAP.
> It was already released as part of Istanbul. So, only back porting to honolulu is needed.

Thank you Ram.
Change has been successfully merged by Pamela Dragosh
